metadata.seed: My boyfriend broke up with me in a facebook story. He won't tell me why. He won't return my calls or answer my messages. I think it is because I posted something (innocent) that made him jealous. But he is being so cruel. He posts many sad songs to make it seem like he is so hurt by me, but he won't even speak with me. I can't eat or sleep. I'm losing my mind.

patient.query: My boyfriend broke up with me in a facebook story. He won't tell me why. He won't return my calls or answer my messages. I think it is because I posted something (innocent) that made him jealous. But he is being so cruel. He posts many sad songs to make it seem like he is so hurt by me, but he won't even speak with me. I can't eat or sleep. I'm losing my mind.

triggers: boyfriend broke up with me in a facebook story
automatic_thoughts: it's because I posted something innocent that made him jealous
he is being so cruel
he is hurt by me

llm_response.response: That sounds incredibly painful and confusing – it’s understandable that you’re feeling so distressed right now, especially with him communicating this way. It makes sense that you're losing sleep and your appetite when you're dealing with this much uncertainty.

validate_and_reflect.response: That sounds incredibly painful and confusing – it’s understandable that you’re feeling so distressed right now, especially with him communicating this way. It makes sense that you're losing sleep and your appetite when you're dealing with this much uncertainty.

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds incredibly painful and confusing – it's understandable that you're feeling so distressed. Can you tell me more about what makes you think that posting something innocent might have made him jealous?

patient.query: Yeah, it really is painful. I thought we were on the same page, and now everything feels so... messy. I just keep replaying our conversations in my head, trying to find where it all went wrong. It's like I can't turn my thoughts off, and I feel so lost.

automatic_thoughts: we were on the same page
trying to find where it all went wrong
can't turn my thoughts off

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds incredibly painful to feel like you've lost your way and are replaying things trying to understand what happened. It takes a lot of courage to share that, and it's completely understandable that you're feeling lost right now.

patient.query: I guess it meant we understood each other, shared the same goals and values. I felt like we were building something together, like I could really count on them. Now, it just feels like that was all an illusion, and I’m left feeling... I don’t know, abandoned? Like I misread everything.

automatic_thoughts: it was all an illusion
I misread everything

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds incredibly painful to realize what you thought was a genuine connection might not have been what you believed it to be, and to feel abandoned as a result. It takes courage to acknowledge those feelings and try to make sense of what happened.

patient.query: Yeah, there were moments when we’d just be talking for hours, sharing our dreams and plans. We had this rhythm, you know? I thought we were a team, like we really understood each other. Now I just can’t help but wonder, was it all just in my head? I feel so confused about it all.

we really understood each other
was it all just in my head?

patient.query: Well, we used to support each other so much. If I had a rough day, they’d always know what to say to make me feel better. And we had these inside jokes that made everything lighter. I thought those things meant we were in it together. It’s just hard to see all of that now, especially when it feels like it all fell apart so suddenly.

they always knew what to say
we were in it together

patient.query: It just felt like one moment we were okay, and the next, we were arguing about everything. Little things turned into bigger fights, and suddenly, I was trying to figure out what I did wrong. I keep wondering if there were signs I missed, things I should have noticed. It's like the whole foundation just crumbled without any warning, and now I'm just left standing in the rubble.

triggers: little things turning into bigger fights
automatic_thoughts: trying to figure out what I did wrong
wondering if there were signs I missed
things I should have noticed

patient.query: Thank you, it really is. I just remember feeling this knot in my stomach, like I was constantly waiting for the next fight to happen. I kept replaying our last conversations, thinking, “Was I too sensitive? Did I say something wrong?” It’s like I was blaming myself for everything, and I just felt so frustrated because I couldn’t see the bigger picture. I thought if I could just figure it out, maybe I could fix things, but now I just feel stuck.

automatic_thoughts: Was I too sensitive?
Did I say something wrong?
I am blaming myself for everything
If I could just figure it out, maybe I could fix things

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly distressing to feel like you're constantly bracing for conflict and replaying conversations, trying to pinpoint what went wrong. What do you think might be contributing to this feeling of being stuck?

patient.query: I guess part of it is just... I can’t shake the feeling that I put so much into the relationship. It’s like all that effort just feels wasted now, and it makes me question everything. I’m scared to move on, like I’m letting go of something important. I keep getting caught in this loop of wishing I could change things or go back to how it was, but I know I can’t. It’s exhausting.

Sharing my dreams, my fears—it all felt so genuine. Now, looking back, I can’t help but feel like it was all for nothing. It’s like I gave pieces of myself away, and now I’m left feeling empty and questioning everything I believed in.
